name: Get Payin Report
summary: Returns the pay-in (add-fund) report for an account over a date range. A POST call whose jData JSON body carries the account id and a from/to date window; the success response is a JSON array of transaction records, each with a transaction reference number, status and amount.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

response_fields.name: actid
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: trans_ref_num
response_fields.description: transaction reference number (number which defines each transaction).

response_fields.name: tran_status
response_fields.description: This is used to indicate the status of transaction.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: Error message; present in a failure response (e.g. session expired). Not listed in the doc's response table but appears in the failure sample.

notes:
- The success response is a JSON ARRAY of transaction objects (one per pay-in), not a single object.
- The doc's response-table "Possible value" column lists ADD_FUND_ST_COMPLETE_STR for tran_status (the value is flattened across three OCR rows ADD_FUND_S / T_COMPLETE / _STR but reads as one token on the page image), while the sample success body shows tran_status as the literal string "Complete". Treat ADD_FUND_ST_COMPLETE_STR as the documented status constant; real payloads may use a short form like "Complete".
- stat possible values are inferred from the samples ("Ok" on success, "Not_Ok" on failure); the doc text only says "success or failure indication".
- from_date / to_date are mandatory but their exact format is not given in the doc (the sample sends them empty "").
- jKey (session token from login) is passed as a query parameter alongside jData, not inside the jData JSON.
- The request jData carries only actid / from_date / to_date — there is no uid field here (unlike the neighbouring FundsPayOutReq).
- BaseURL in the curl example resolves to https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI.
issues: The cleaned text flattens the response table; field-to-value binding was confirmed against the page-66 image. The only request jData fields shown are actid/from_date/to_date (no uid in this report's body, unlike the neighbouring FundsPayOutReq). The tran_status possible-value cell is split across three OCR lines but reads as a single token ADD_FUND_ST_COMPLETE_STR on the image. emsg is not in the doc's response table but appears in the failure sample, so it is included as a response field with a provenance note.
